pre_checks: Confirm root-cause candidate DC-FW-01 is present in enterprise graph memory.
Validate impacted diagrams before any change: branch_topology, datacenter_topology, app_db_topology, shared_services_topology.
Confirm RCA source and alert timeline freshness before remediation.
Run read-only reachability checks across diagram boundaries first.
triage_steps: Focus initial investigation on node 'DC-FW-01' in datacenter_topology.
Inspect core switch interface error counters and spanning-tree topology.
Verify inter-service communication across fabric via traceroute.
Check data-centre firewall deny logs for new ACL hits.
Confirm power and cooling alarms are not contributing to device instability.
validation_steps: Confirm packet-drop counters on the affected firewall interface have stopped incrementing.
Verify traffic flows that were blocked are now passing the firewall policy.
Check the firewall deny log to confirm no residual ACL hits on legitimate traffic.
Confirm the security team has reviewed and approved the policy change before closure.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: DC-FW-01, DC-CORE-SW-01, APP-LB-01, APP-01, DB-MASTER, IAM-01….
Validate according to SOP-DC-FW-001 (Datacenter Firewall Packet Drop and Link Error Response) validation section [KB-SOP-DC-FW-001-006].
Validate according to RUNBOOK-ENT-XDIAG-001 (Enterprise Cross-Diagram Incident Validation Runbook) validation section [KB-RUNBOOK-ENT-XDIAG-001-006].
remediation_steps: Review the firewall policy change log for any recently added or modified deny rules.
Identify affected traffic flows from packet-drop counters on the suspect interface.
Confirm the ACL or policy rule causing drops — do not remove rules without change approval.
Apply firewall policy correction only within an approved maintenance window.
After policy update, confirm packet-drop counters stop incrementing and traffic restores.
Document the change and update the CMDB record for the affected firewall.
Coordinate recovery across all affected diagrams in order: branch_topology → datacenter_topology → app_db_topology → shared_services_topology.
post_checks: Confirm packet-drop counters on the affected firewall interface have stopped incrementing.
Verify traffic flows that were blocked are now passing the firewall policy.
Check the firewall deny log to confirm no residual ACL hits on legitimate traffic.
Confirm the security team has reviewed and approved the policy change before closure.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: DC-FW-01, DC-CORE-SW-01, APP-LB-01, APP-01, DB-MASTER, IAM-01….

Validate according to RUNBOOK-ENT-XDIAG-001 (Enterprise Cross-Diagram Incident Validation Runbook) validation section [KB-RUNBOOK-ENT-XDIAG-001-006].
do_not_execute_if: Do not execute changes if root cause DC-FW-01 is not confirmed by graph evidence.
Do not execute if a rollback owner and maintenance window are not approved.
Do not execute if alert timestamps are stale or no longer match the active incident.
Do not execute cross-domain changes without NOC and owning team approval.
rollback_or_safety_notes: Capture running configs on all affected devices before any change.
Coordinate maintenance windows across affected sites — changes here have cross-domain blast radius.
Confirm a rollback plan is in place for each affected diagram domain.
Notify NOC before modifying WAN or shared-services components.
Stage changes in the least-critical diagram first; validate before proceeding.
escalation_recommendation: This incident spans 4 topology domains — immediate escalation to senior network engineering is recommended. Escalate to network engineering team if root-cause node cannot be restored within 30 minutes, or if the incident spans more than 3 topology domains. Notify on-call SRE if customer-facing services remain impacted.
